steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence needs a verb for the subject "an abstract cubist painting." Two things must be right: the verb must agree in number with that subject, and the tense must reflect that the painting entered the Western art canon at some point in the past with relevance that continues to the present.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘were entering’: plural verb. "A painting were entering" violates subject-verb agreement — a singular subject requires a singular verb. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘enter’: a base/plural present form. It does not agree with the singular subject an abstract cubist painting, and it also fails to express the completed action with present relevance. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘has entered’: singular present perfect. Agrees with the singular subject and correctly signals a completed past action whose result still matters now — the painting has become part of the canon. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘have entered’: present perfect but plural. "A painting have entered" is grammatically incorrect — singular subject, plural verb. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
